item.title: Implementing Custom Memory Allocator in C++
item.description: In this post, I elaborate on my implementation of a memory allocator in C++. The malloc, calloc, realloc, and free functions are implemented and discussed. The aim is to provide educational content on how these functions work under the hood. Not all aspects are taken into account in this custom implementation; however, this provides a sufficient introduction to memory management in C++. Note that standard allocators like glibc malloc are highly optimized and have been battle-tested for decades.

item.title: A Short Overview of CoW Protocol
item.description:
What is CoW Protocol? Link to heading

Meta-DEX aggregation protocol that uses trade intents and batch auctions to find users better prices for trading crypto assets.

Relies on third parties known as “solvers” to find the best execution paths for trade intents — signed messages that specify conditions for executing transactions on Ethereum and EVM-compatible chains.

Core Architecture Link to heading

Solver Process Link to heading

  1. Receives user intent → Groups intents with others in a batch
  2. Finds Coincidence of Wants (CoW) → To offer optimal price over ANY onchain liquidity
  3. Executes batch → Settles all trades at uniform clearing price

Liquidity Sources Link to heading

  • AMMs (Uniswap, Sushiswap, Balancer, Curve, etc.)
  • DEX Aggregators (1inch, Paraswap, Matcha, etc.)
  • Private Market Makers

Result: CoW Swap is an aggregator of aggregators.

item.title: ENDGAME: How we use ZK to secure MegaETH
Implementing Custom Slab Allocator with C++
item.description:
implementing a slab allocator: high-performance memory management Link to heading

Slab allocators are one of the most efficient memory allocation strategies for high-frequency allocations of fixed-size objects. Originally developed by Sun Microsystems for the Solaris kernel, slab allocators have found their way into many high-performance systems.

What is a Slab Allocator? Link to heading

A slab allocator pre-allocates memory in fixed-size chunks (slabs) and maintains free lists for each size class. When you request memory, it simply pops an object from the appropriate free list - an O(1) operation.

item.title: about
item.description:
Hi, I’m Memduh, a systems programmer and low-level developer passionate about computer architecture, FPGA design, and operating systems.

Freshly graduated from Middle East Technical University with a B.S. in Electrical and Electronics Engineering. I am interested in computer architecture, FPGA design, systems programming, embedded systems, and high frequency systems. I work with register-level programming, Verilog HDL, C++ and Assembly programming, operating systems, memory management, and embedded development.

GitHub: @memduhb
Twitter: @0xmemduh

item.title: Consensus Protocols
item.description:
Consensus Protocols Link to heading

Consensus protocols are the backbone of distributed systems, enabling multiple nodes to agree on a single state or value despite potential failures and network partitions. From traditional distributed databases to modern blockchain networks, consensus protocols ensure reliability and consistency in decentralized environments.

“12 Angry Men” film shows 12 jurors deciding the future of an 18-year-old boy accused of murder. For a decision to emerge from the room, unanimity must be achieved.
